summary: Defines the Store interface and all repository interfaces/domain types; implements sqliteStore factory via Open()

requires: writable filesystem path for the SQLite database file
modernc.org/sqlite driver registered via blank import
guarantees: Open returns a fully migrated Store or closes the DB and returns an error — never a partially-initialized Store
MaxOpenConns is capped at 1, serializing all DB access through a single connection
~ in dbPath is expanded before use; parent directories are created with mode 0700 if absent

effects: filesystem: creates parent directory for DB file (os.MkdirAll, mode 0700)
runs DDL migrations on first open (delegates to runMigrations in migrations.go)
state: sqliteStore holds a *sql.DB and four eagerly-initialized repository instances; single-connection pool means the Store is not safe for concurrent open/close lifecycle management from multiple goroutines, though individual repository queries go through database/sql's own concurrency handling

observations.text: Store is a pure interface — callers depend only on Store, ChannelRepository, MessageRepository, MessageSummaryRepository, and SyncStateRepository interfaces. The concrete sqliteStore and its repo fields are unexported, making substitution in tests trivial.

db.SetMaxOpenConns(1) intentionally serializes SQLite access. This avoids SQLITE_BUSY errors from concurrent writers but means all repository operations queue behind a single connection. High-throughput callers will see latency spikes.
Open runs migrations synchronously before returning the Store. Any migration failure closes the DB and surfaces the error, preventing callers from using a partially-migrated schema.

This is the highest fan-in file in the storage package — all external callers (refresh pipeline, summarizer, source system, TUI, CLI orchestrator) receive a Store from Open() and interact only with the interfaces defined here.
expandPath only handles the ~/ prefix form; a bare ~ without a trailing slash is returned unchanged. This matches the pattern noted in config.go and is consistent across the codebase.

Domain types (Channel, Message, MessageSummary, SyncState) are defined here alongside their repository interfaces — all repository contracts are co-located in a single file, making this the authoritative schema for the storage package's public surface.
